description: §60.4/§60.9 — a `<request api="X">` naming an endpoint declared in no in-scope `<api>` (here a misspelling) fires E-API-ENDPOINT-UNKNOWN (typer, W3). The endpoint name is resolved against the `<api>` endpoint set; an unknown name has nothing to bind the typed fetch to.
rationale: §60.4: `<request api="endpointName">` SHALL resolve `endpointName` against the in-scope `<api>` endpoints; unknown → E-API-ENDPOINT-UNKNOWN. Because the endpoint never resolves, no request-shape check runs, so E-API-REQ-SHAPE-MISMATCH stays silent.
